XGSetPaletteHeader

Manually creates a palette resource.

VOID WINAPI XGSetPaletteHeader(
  D3DPALETTESIZE Size,
  IDirect3DPalette8* pPalette,
  UINT Data
);

Parameters

Size
[in] Specifies the number of entries to be placed in the palette. Any of the following constants are valid values:
D3DPALETTE_256
D3DPALETTE_128
D3DPALETTE_64
D3DPALETTE_32
pPalette
[in, out] Address of the IDirect3DPalette8 interface, representing the created palette resource that is to be filled.
Data
[in] Offset to the data held by this resource.

Return Values

None.

Remarks

All resources, including palettes, include a header that contains all of the information needed by the driver to process the resource and a data allocation that contains information used by the GPU. The resource headers are used only by the driver and so should be allocated from normal cached memory. The header contains all of the information needed by the driver to set up the hardware so that it can use the information stored in the data allocation.

Unless the title is manually creating a resource, it is recommended that the title use IDirect3DDevice8::CreatePalette.

After calling XGSetPaletteHeader, it is necessary to call IDirect3DResource8::Register in order to register the memory address of the resource.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in xgraphics.h.
  Import Library: Use xgraphics.lib.
